title: Streaming Sitemap XML, Index Graphs, and Admission Bounds · Contract Triage
summary: A preserved Go crawl trace contains one plausible scope, URL, robots, transport, body, tokenizer, goquery, frontier, goroutine, Colly callback, report, security, or shutdown defect; identify the first broken invariant before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in streaming bounded sitemap graph and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use encoding/xml tokens to validate namespace, root, UTF-8, loc, escaping, depth, counts, bytes, and trailing content without whole-tree allocation.
Model sitemap indexes as a bounded directed graph with unique identities, depth limits, cycle detection, and stable traversal.
Apply protocol location rules plus authorization, URL, robots, redirect, DNS, and SSRF policy to every sitemap and page entry.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and Go Order the regression proof · Debug Sitemap Provenance
steps.instruction:
Order the Sitemap Provenance Authorized site auditing and Go HTTP evidence cycle for this debug task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Authorized site auditing and Go Order the regression proof · Debug Sitemap Provenance.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Sitemap Provenance assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Authorized site auditing and Go Order the regression proof · Debug Sitemap Provenance, keep Sitemap Provenance connected to executable Go authorization, URL, robots, response, tree, frontier, goroutine, report, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.text: A debug Go team handles case 1596 during a mixed-origin fairness and race rehearsal. Build deterministic Go evidence to stream bounded sitemap XML and index graphs through host, scope, hint, and provenance admission; every URL, body, tree, record, frontier transition, and report row retains causal identity; then change one tokenizer, goquery, selector, domain constructor, record, link, frontier, or trap case and retain rejected evidence. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser-service, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, x/net/html, goquery, Colly, files, processes, race, load, assistive-technology review, containers, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Retain source digest, index chain, entry position, raw loc, resolved key, decision, and rejection cause.
